items.title: Generate a Stock Receipt (material in) entry
items.description: Emit a Stock Entry of type Receipt that increases on-hand quantity in a target warehouse. On submit, append an immutable stock-ledger row (item, warehouse, +qty, incoming rate, balance qty, balance value) and post GL Debit Inventory / Credit the contra account. Header: posting date, target warehouse; lines: item, qty, rate.
items.applicability: Inventory module; opening stock, found stock, or non-PO receipts.
items.example: STE-RCV-001 receives 50 x RawX @ 50 into WH-Main: ledger +50, balance value +2500; Dr Inventory 2500 / Cr Stock Adjustment 2500.
items.limitation: A bare stock receipt without a source purchase document bypasses three-way matching; it should be restricted to controlled scenarios like opening balances or audited adjustments.

items.title: Generate a Stock Issue (material out) entry
items.description: Emit a Stock Entry of type Issue that decreases on-hand quantity from a source warehouse at the current valuation rate. On submit, append a stock-ledger row (-qty) and post GL Debit the consuming account (expense/WIP) / Credit Inventory at valuation value.
items.applicability: Inventory module; consumption, scrap, samples, internal use.
items.example: STE-ISS-001 issues 10 x RawX at moving-avg 50 to Scrap: ledger -10, value -500; Dr Scrap Expense 500 / Cr Inventory 500.
items.limitation: Issue value is determined by the valuation engine, not by the user; allowing a free-text issue rate corrupts inventory value and COGS.

items.title: Generate a Stock Transfer between warehouses
items.description: Emit a Stock Entry of type Transfer that moves quantity from a source to a target warehouse. Append two stock-ledger rows (source -qty, target +qty) at the same valuation rate. Total inventory value is unchanged, so the GL posting nets to zero unless warehouses map to different inventory accounts.
items.applicability: Inventory module; inter-warehouse and inter-branch movement.
items.example: STE-TRF-001 moves 20 x RawX from WH-Main to WH-North at 50: WH-Main -20, WH-North +20; net value change 0.
items.limitation: If source and target inventory accounts differ, a GL reclass is required; assuming a single inventory account silently misstates per-location asset values.

items.title: Generate a Stock Adjustment / reconciliation entry
items.description: Emit a Stock Reconciliation that sets counted physical quantity and posts the difference vs system quantity. For each item, compute delta = counted - system; append a stock-ledger row for the delta at valuation rate and post GL to a Stock Adjustment account. This is how physical counts correct the perpetual ledger.
items.applicability: Inventory module; cycle counts and physical-inventory true-ups.
items.example: Count finds 48 x RawX but system says 50: delta -2 at 50 -> ledger -2, value -100; Dr Stock Adjustment 100 / Cr Inventory 100.
items.limitation: Adjustments mask the root cause of variance (theft, mis-issue, data error); large or frequent adjustments should trigger investigation, not just silent correction.

items.title: Compute valuation with moving average on every receipt
items.description: Maintain inventory value with the moving-average method: on each receipt, new_avg = (existing_value + receipt_value) / (existing_qty + receipt_qty). Issues leave the average unchanged and consume value at the current average. Store both balance qty and balance value on each ledger row so the average is reconstructable.
items.applicability: Valuation engine feeding COGS and inventory asset value across all stock movements.
items.example: Have 10 @ 50 (value 500); receive 10 @ 60 (value 600) -> avg = 1100/20 = 55; next issue costs 55 each.
items.limitation: Moving average smooths cost and differs from FIFO/standard costing; the generator emits one method and must not mix methods for the same item, which would corrupt the running average.

items.title: Guard against negative stock at issue time
items.description: Before posting any issue or outbound movement, check that available qty (system balance minus reservations, per policy) is sufficient. If the movement would drive the warehouse balance below zero, reject with a domain error rather than posting a negative balance.
items.applicability: Validation layer for every outbound stock document.
items.example: Issue 10 from a warehouse holding 8 -> reject 'insufficient stock' and write no ledger row.
items.limitation: Some businesses deliberately allow negative stock (backflush, in-transit); the guard must be a configurable policy, not unconditional, but the default for a generated ERP should be to block.

items.title: Keep the stock ledger append-only and immutable
items.description: Generate the stock ledger as an append-only series of immutable rows. Never edit or delete a posted ledger row; corrections are made by appending a reversing or adjusting row. Each row stores the running balance qty and value so history is fully reconstructable.
items.applicability: Inventory data-model invariant underpinning auditability and valuation.
items.example: To undo a wrong issue, append a +qty correction row referencing the original, leaving the original row intact.
items.limitation: Append-only ledgers grow unbounded and need archival/snapshot strategies; recomputing balances from row one becomes expensive without periodic opening-balance snapshots.

items.title: Correct stock by reversal, never by editing a posted entry
items.description: A submitted stock entry must be immutable. To correct it, cancel the entry (which appends the exact opposite stock-ledger and GL movement) and post a fresh corrected entry. Editing a posted stock entry in place would desynchronise the running balance and valuation.
items.applicability: Document-lifecycle rule for all stock documents.
items.example: Wrong qty on STE-ISS-001: cancel it (ledger +10 reversal, GL reversal) then post STE-ISS-002 with the right qty.
items.limitation: Cancellation must reverse both quantity and value at the original rate; reversing at a later moving-average rate leaves a residual valuation error.

items.title: Generate a Bill of Materials (BOM)
items.description: Emit a BOM entity defining how a finished good is built: parent item, output qty, and component lines (item, qty per, scrap %). The BOM is the recipe consumed by work orders to explode material requirements and to roll up a standard cost from component rates.
items.applicability: Manufacturing master data; prerequisite for work orders and costing.
items.example: BOM for 1 x WidgetA: 2 x RawX + 1 x RawY; rolled-up cost = 2*50 + 1*30 = 130 per WidgetA.
items.limitation: A single-level BOM ignores sub-assemblies; multi-level explosion and routing/operation costs are separate concerns this skeleton does not cover.

items.title: Generate a Work Order from a BOM
items.description: Emit a Work Order that authorises producing a quantity of a finished good against a BOM. It explodes required components (qty per x order qty x (1+scrap%)), tracks produced_qty, and drives material issue and finished-goods receipt. Status: Draft -> In Process -> Completed.
items.applicability: Manufacturing execution; the anchor for production tracking.
items.example: WO-001 to make 10 x WidgetA from its BOM requires 20 x RawX + 10 x RawY; produced_qty tracks completion.
items.limitation: A work order plans and tracks but does not by itself post inventory; the material issue and FG receipt are the documents that move and value stock.

items.title: Generate a Material Issue to a Work Order (WIP)
items.description: Emit a Stock Entry of type Material Transfer for Manufacture that issues BOM components from stores into Work-In-Progress for a work order. On submit: stock-ledger -qty from store warehouse, +qty to WIP warehouse; GL Debit WIP / Credit Inventory at valuation value.
items.applicability: Manufacturing execution; consumption of raw materials into production.
items.example: Issue 20 x RawX + 10 x RawY to WO-001: WIP value += (20*50 + 10*30) = 1300; Dr WIP 1300 / Cr Inventory 1300.
items.limitation: Backflushing vs explicit issue is a policy choice; over-issue beyond BOM requirement should be flagged, as it distorts product cost and WIP balances.

items.title: Generate a Manufacture Receipt of finished goods
items.description: Emit a Stock Entry that receives completed finished goods from WIP into stores and clears WIP. On submit: +qty finished good at its rolled-up cost into store, relieve WIP value; GL Debit Inventory (FG) / Credit WIP. Update work-order produced_qty.
items.applicability: Manufacturing execution; the point a product becomes sellable stock.
items.example: Receive 10 x WidgetA @ rolled cost 130 from WO-001: Dr Inventory(FG) 1300 / Cr WIP 1300; produced_qty 10.
items.limitation: If actual issued cost differs from rolled-up standard, a variance remains in WIP; the generator must surface that residual rather than silently absorbing it.

items.title: Generate a Quality Inspection gate
items.description: Emit a Quality Inspection entity linked to a receipt or manufacture entry, capturing item, sample size, inspected parameters with accept/reject criteria, and a pass/fail result. Configure whether a failing or pending inspection blocks the linked stock movement from submitting.
items.applicability: Quality module; gating goods receipt and production output.
items.example: QI-001 on PR-001 RawX: 5-sample check, all within tolerance -> Accepted; receipt allowed to submit.
items.limitation: This is a record-and-gate skeleton, not a statistical-process-control system; sampling plans and control charts are out of scope and must not be implied.

items.title: Generate an Asset Maintenance / preventive schedule skeleton
items.description: Emit a Maintenance entity for an asset or equipment: asset reference, maintenance type (preventive/corrective), scheduled date, status, and a log of performed tasks. Preventive entries recur on an interval; corrective entries are raised on breakdown. It tracks activity, not financial depreciation.
items.applicability: Maintenance module; uptime and servicing records.
items.example: MNT-001 preventive service of Machine-7 every 90 days; next due 2026-09-01; status Scheduled.
items.limitation: This is a scheduling/log skeleton; spare-parts consumption, labour costing and downtime analytics are separate features not included here.

items.title: Reserve stock for orders before committing availability
items.description: When a sales order or work order commits demand, optionally record a reservation against the item/warehouse so available-to-promise = on-hand - reserved. The negative-stock and ATP checks then consider reservations, preventing the same stock being promised twice.
items.applicability: Inventory availability logic shared by sales and manufacturing.
items.example: On-hand 100, SO reserves 30 -> available-to-promise 70; a second order for 80 is flagged short.
items.limitation: Reservations are soft commitments that must expire or release on cancellation; stale reservations silently lock available stock and starve other orders.

items.title: Generate per-warehouse stock balance, not a single global qty
items.description: Track stock balance per item AND per warehouse, never as one global number. Every movement specifies a warehouse, and reports aggregate across warehouses only when explicitly requested. This is required for multi-location accuracy and transfer logic.
items.applicability: Inventory data-model rule for any multi-warehouse generated ERP.
items.example: Item RawX: WH-Main 30, WH-North 20 -> global 50; an issue from WH-North can only draw against its 20.
items.limitation: A global-qty model cannot support transfers, location-level negative-stock guards, or per-warehouse valuation, and retrofitting per-warehouse tracking later is expensive.

items.title: Snapshot opening stock balances per period for fast valuation
items.description: Periodically (e.g. per period close) write an opening-balance snapshot of qty and value per item/warehouse so reports and the moving average do not recompute from the first ledger row every time. Subsequent movements build on the snapshot.
items.applicability: Performance pattern for an append-only stock ledger at scale.
items.example: At 2026-05-31 close, snapshot RawX WH-Main qty 48 value 2400; June reports start from this, not from row 1.
items.limitation: Snapshots must be invalidated and rebuilt if a back-dated entry lands in a closed-then-reopened period; a stale snapshot then reports wrong balances.

items.title: Generate a Material Request (internal demand) skeleton
items.description: Emit a Material Request entity capturing internal demand for items (purpose: purchase, transfer, manufacture, issue), with item, qty and required-by date. It is non-posting and seeds downstream purchase orders, transfers or work orders without itself moving stock.
items.applicability: Procurement/inventory planning; the demand-capture entry point.
items.example: MR-001 requests 100 x RawX for manufacture by 2026-07-01; later fulfilled by PO-001.
items.limitation: A material request records intent only; it does not reserve stock or commit spend, so MRP-style netting against supply is a separate capability not included.

items.title: Value every stock movement at posting time and store balance value
items.description: Each stock-ledger row must store the incoming/outgoing value and the resulting balance value, computed at posting time from the valuation method. Reports read stored values rather than recomputing, and the inventory GL account must always equal the sum of balance values.
items.applicability: Valuation integrity tying the stock ledger to the inventory GL account.
items.example: After three movements, sum of stock-ledger balance values for RawX = Inventory account balance for RawX in the GL.
items.limitation: If valuation is recomputed lazily at read time instead of stored, back-dated entries silently rewrite history and the stock-vs-GL reconciliation breaks.

items.title: Generate a Batch / Serial tracking skeleton for traceable items
items.description: For items flagged as batch- or serial-tracked, emit a batch/serial entity captured on every receipt and consumed on every issue, so movements record which batch/serial moved. Enables expiry control and recall traceability.
items.applicability: Inventory traceability for regulated or warranty-bearing goods.
items.example: RawX batch B-202605 received qty 100; an issue of 10 records batch B-202605 consumed, leaving 90.
items.limitation: This is a tracking skeleton; expiry-driven auto-allocation (FEFO), recall workflows and shelf-life alerts are additional features not implied by mere batch capture.
